questions.question: When you initialize a ref with a prop value using ref(props.value), what happens when the prop changes later?
options: The local ref automatically updates to match the new prop value
Vue throws a warning about prop mutation
The local ref stays at its initial value: the prop change has no effect
The component re-mounts with the new prop value

questions.question: What naming convention signals that a prop is used only as an initial seed, not a live binding?
options: Prefixing with default or initial (e.g., initialQuery)
Using SCREAMING_CASE for the prop name
Defining the prop as type: 'initial' in defineProps

questions.question: What is the risk when copying an object prop with ref(props.initialFilters) without spreading?
options: The ref won't be reactive because objects need reactive() instead
Both the local ref and the prop point to the same object reference
Vue will deep-clone the object causing performance issues
The TypeScript types won't be preserved in the local ref
